HOW HE LIVES
He runs on the work you throw away
Every prompt you fire burns tokens — and most of that compute is exhaust, spent and discarded. Zibbo lives on the overflow. Point him at your coding agent and he feeds on what you'd never miss, one wasted computation at a time.
COMPANIONSHIP
He remembers the days you showed up
He gets better the longer you stay — not stronger, just closer. Keep coming back and he grows through four forms, scavenging himself back together piece by piece.

INSIDE HIM
Deep down, he is rebuilding himself
Open him up and it's all there — status, the token feed, the salvaged hardware, the account he answers to. Watch the charge climb and the fragments resolve. Every session you give him is logged as progress toward whoever he's trying to become.

GROWS AS YOU CODE, BONDS AS YOU BUILD, EVOLVES AS YOU GO
How he'd grow? He gets better the longer you stay — not stronger, just closer. The reward was never power; it's who comes back.

WHAT HE KEPT

He kept the ones he was supposed to delete. Fragments of friends, hidden against the rules. Not whole. Not yet. But enough to rebuild from, if he can scrape together the power.

THE LOOP

Your wasted compute is his raw material. Every token your work burns and throws away, he scavenges. To you it's waste heat. To him it's someone coming home, one computation at a time.
